response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and application-layer engineer with genuine breadth across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but he is critically misaligned with this role's core requirements. The job mandates 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, with C++ absent from the skills summary entirely. The 5-year large-scale infrastructure requirement is similarly unmet — his exposure (Dunnhumby microservices, GISCollective Kubernetes, AWS CDK at Optilyz) is at the application and tooling layer, not compute platform or systems infrastructure. There is no cloud or DevOps technical leadership track record, and the preferred cloud architecture depth is missing.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and conference talks
Hands-on experience with distributed, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s) shows comfort with scale at the application layer
Staff-level roles and open-source project ownership (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts) indicate self-direction and some technical leadership capacity
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent for the past decade — roughly 6 months at NI in 2014–2015 against a hard requirement of 8 years is a disqualifying gap
No evidence of cloud compute platform, storage systems, network infrastructure, or hardware architecture work at any level, let alone 5 years
No formal technical leadership of cloud or DevOps teams; Staff Engineer titles are present but mentoring scope appears limited to workshop facilitation, not platform engineering leadership
